facade_description: 227 Augusta Ave presents a commercial street frontage within the Kensington Market HCD. The street frontage spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys beneath a cross-gable, consistent with its commercial typology. The frontage is defined primarily by brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade is organized as a storefront base with 2 upper-window bays, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance. HCD note: The property has value as an early-twentieth-century vernacular style two-storey commercial addition to a nineteenth-century residential structure. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 190
